challenged_assumptions: A1: AC9 is not just "serialization unproven"; there is no existing exhaustive baseline. Current tests only assert Minecraft contentSurface and font-size floors (app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts:4-33), while AC9 requires byte-identical Chakra output for all themes (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:159-160).

A2: The generated TypeScript registry is not a neutral implementation detail. It becomes the source of AppThemeName, APP_THEMES, and THEME_FONTS (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19,39), so generator behavior leaks into type checking, runtime theme lookup, profile UI ordering, and font loading.

A3: The proposal itself acknowledges the prebuild gate misses make test-unit (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:57); the repo confirms make test-unit runs npx vitest directly (Makefile:107-109) and npm test:unit is only vitest run (app/package.json:10). That makes stale registry state a normal development path, not an edge case.

A4: Function-free manifests are already under pressure from current assets: banner SVGs embed theme-specific colors directly (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:179-292) and backgrounds are handcrafted strings in theme.ts (app/src/lib/theme.ts:376-581). A future token-derived banner/pattern would violate the manifest model during this epic, not after it.

A5: The visualStyle decomposition is riskier than described because existing behavior is not decomposed uniformly: banners exist for all five styles (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:179-292), contentPanel only has a pixel branch (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:136-153), icon selection is pixel/toy/default (app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx:12-73), and isFunTheme is a separate derived rule (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:345-346).

A6: Making fun an explicit manifest flag introduces a second source of truth for a value currently derived from visualStyle (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:345-346). If a manifest chooses toy-like treatments but omits fun, or sets fun on a flat treatment, consumers cannot tell whether the manifest or treatment table owns the behavior.

A7: On-demand extendTheme changes the identity contract of getChakraTheme. Today getChakraTheme returns a prebuilt object stored in APP_THEMES (app/src/lib/theme.ts:677-750), and useAppTheme passes that into ChakraProvider on each render (app/src/hooks/useMoodTheme.tsx:54-60; app/pages/_app.tsx:35-40). Rebuilding on demand silently changes referential stability.
paradigm_risks: The package-by-feature island contradicts the explored app architecture more than it composes with it. Exploration describes the current system as layered/n-tier with types, domain, persistence, hooks, and shell separated (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/exploration.json:5-15), but the proposal permits types -> registry and _document -> registry cross-boundary imports (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:49-51).

The proposed themes package is not self-contained at the architectural level. The plan still modifies lib/theme.ts, types/index.ts, useThemeStyles.ts, ThemeIcon.tsx, _document.tsx, profile.tsx, i18n.ts, package.json, scripts, and tests (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:25-33), so package-by-feature only applies to future additions, not to the epic's actual coupling.

The codebase precedent for generated data is not package-by-feature. Exploration identifies generate-avatar-manifest.mjs writing src/data/avatarManifest.json and consumers casting locally (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/exploration.json:75-83); the proposal instead commits a generated TS module under app/src/themes that exports runtime data, types, and font metadata (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19).

For this scale, the proposal creates a large local architecture around five themes and six public API consumers. The existing public theme API is consumed by only six files plus one test (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:44-45), while the proposed design adds schema, builder, three treatment modules, registry, index, generator, validation gate, and five manifests (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:61-78).

The current theme concern is cross-cutting presentation, not a vertical feature. ChakraProvider consumes theme at the app shell (app/pages/_app.tsx:35-40), Layout consumes style derivations (app/src/components/Layout.tsx:51), ThemeIcon consumes visual style (app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx:92-100), profile lists APP_THEMES (app/pages/profile.tsx:221-264), and storage normalizes theme ids (app/src/lib/storage.ts:61-76).
replaceability_gaps: schema.ts cannot be deleted and rewritten independently because ThemeManifest is the contract for manifests, registry, buildChakraTheme, useThemeStyles, ThemeIcon, profile labels, font generation, and validation (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37,49). Cross-field invariants are explicitly outside zod and live in tests (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37), so schema is not the sole source of truth.

buildChakraTheme is not replaceable without preserving createTheme's exact Chakra object shape. The current createTheme emits config, colors, semanticTokens, fonts, fontSizes, radii, global body styles, and component defaults (app/src/lib/theme.ts:117-203), and _app requires a Chakra ThemeOverride through ChakraProvider (app/pages/_app.tsx:40).

treatments/elevation.ts is coupled to useThemeStyles' exact return values, not just to abstract names. Existing card/button/nav/surface/contentPanel behavior is spread directly into Layout and the home page (app/src/components/Layout.tsx:78-88,340-350; app/pages/index.tsx:68-80), so rewriting the treatment table can break call sites without touching their code.

treatments/iconSets.ts is coupled to ThemeIcon's fallback semantics. The current ICON_MAP is keyed by icon name with pixel/toy/default variants and returns null for unknown names (app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx:12-119); a new icon-set shape would force changes in ThemeIcon and any non-React callers of getThemeIconId.

registry.generated.ts is not replaceable glue; it becomes the source of the AppThemeName union, APP_THEMES runtime record, and THEME_FONTS (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19,39). That couples generator output to types/index.ts, lib/theme re-exports, _document, storage normalization, profile iteration, and tests.

themes/index.ts is not a thin replaceable facade if it owns on-demand build behavior and possible caching. useAppTheme calls getChakraTheme and getThemeDefinition on every hook use (app/src/hooks/useMoodTheme.tsx:54-60), so index-level changes can alter render-time object identity and activeThemeDefinition semantics.

Manifest files are not isolated data blobs. They must encode old theme.ts token values (app/src/lib/theme.ts:206-675), old visual-style decomposition choices (app/src/lib/theme.ts:683-731), banner SVGs (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:179-292), font loading metadata matching _document (app/pages/_document.tsx:9-11), labels formerly in i18n (app/src/lib/i18n.ts:83-94,545-556,1002-1013), and schema/treatment enum names.
seam_risks: The useThemeStyles contract is internally inconsistent. The proposal says the return shape is unchanged but also says visualStyle is dropped (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:41); the actual hook currently returns visualStyle (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:325-330) and exports ThemeVisualStyle (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:4-6).

The ThemeIcon contract drifts between documents. The spec says ThemeIcon reads manifest.iconSet (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:101), while the proposal says manifest.treatments.iconSet (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:29,37). Different story owners can implement incompatible shapes and still believe they followed the architecture.

contentSurface has no durable ownership. The manifest contract says contentSurface is optional and auto-derived from colorScheme === 'dark' (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37), but consumers are described as reading manifest.contentSurface directly (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:28,39). Current behavior depends on an actual truthy flag at render time (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:340) and tests assert APP_THEMES.minecraft.contentSurface is true (app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts:6-18).

THEME_FONTS is already specified incorrectly. The proposal says the current URL covers DM Serif Display(400), Fredoka(400;600), Nunito(400;500;600;700), Press Start 2P(400), VT323(400) (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:43), but the actual URL includes DM Serif Display ital@0;1, Fredoka 400/500/600/700, Nunito 400/600/700/800, Press Start 2P, and VT323 (app/pages/_document.tsx:9-11).

Registry ordering is an unowned seam. Profile renders Object.values(APP_THEMES) (app/pages/profile.tsx:221), and the current APP_THEMES insertion order is calm, playful, lego, minecraft, flower (app/src/lib/theme.ts:677-733). A filesystem-glob-generated registry can be idempotent while still changing UI order and violating visual parity.

Treatment-selector tables cannot fully encode the old visualStyle coupling without hidden conventions. Existing soft and rounded have banners but empty overlays (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:179-218,27-49,51-85), floral has default icons but floral overlays (app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx:12-73; app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:40-45,71-81,112-125,167-172), and pixel contentPanel is gated separately (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:136-153,340).

AC9 and AC10 can drift independently. buildChakraTheme parity covers Chakra ThemeOverride (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:159-160), while useThemeStyles parity covers BoxProps and banner data URIs (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:161-163). The proposal places these in separate modules and stories (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:227-234), so one can pass while the other regresses.
parallel_hazards: registry.generated.ts is a shared write target for story 2 manifests, story 3 generator, story 4 consumers, and any branch adding/removing a theme. Because it is committed and exports both runtime data and types (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19), parallel branches will create merge conflicts and stale type/runtime assumptions.

Story 1 cannot safely land schema/buildChakraTheme/index before story 3 generator exists if index imports registry.generated.ts. The proposal makes app/src/themes net-new (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/exploration.json:69) and the registry the public API source (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19-20), so partial story order can break compilation.

Story 2 manifests depend on treatment enum names from story 1 and validation semantics from story 3. The manifest treatments field names elevation/iconSet/surfacePattern/banner/overrides/fun are specified in prose (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37), while the old source only has visualStyle values (app/src/lib/theme.ts:683-731); parallel implementation can choose incompatible decompositions.

Story 4 consumers can compile against stale generated output because make test-unit and npm test:unit do not run the generator (Makefile:107-109; app/package.json:10). This directly attacks A3 and makes registry freshness dependent on developer memory outside the build path.

The AC9 baseline is time-sensitive shared state. The spec requires capturing current getChakraTheme output before refactor (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:204-205), but the story split starts with scaffold + schema + builder before migration/parity (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:227-232). Once any story changes theme.ts or createTheme, the baseline is contaminated.

package.json/package-lock become cross-story contention points: D3 adds zod and a prebuild script (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:33), while the repo currently has no zod direct dependency or prebuild script (app/package.json:5-15,16-56). Story owners touching tests/build/deps will collide in the same files.

types/index.ts becomes a shared dependency inversion point. It currently defines AppThemeName locally (app/src/types/index.ts:8); the proposal moves that to generated registry import (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:27,49), so story 1, story 3, storage, and hook changes can block each other through global types.
evolution_triggers: A new theme that wants a token-derived pattern or banner will force a revision of A4. Current banners and backgrounds are static strings with embedded colors (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:179-292; app/src/lib/theme.ts:376-581), while manifests are explicitly function-free and serializable (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:80-93).

A true dark theme with light text and no content panel will challenge the colorScheme => contentSurface rule. The spec says contentSurface auto-derives from colorScheme === 'dark' (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:94), but current Minecraft is a dark background with light surfaces and dark text (app/src/lib/theme.ts:538-545) rather than a general dark-mode model.

A theme needing a non-pixel content panel treatment will expose that contentPanel is not actually composable. Current contentPanel only returns props for pixel (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:136-153), while the proposal only adds contentSurface as a boolean gate, not a content-panel treatment selector (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37,45).

A font requiring italics, variable axes, or exact weight preservation will force THEME_FONTS redesign. The current Google Fonts link already uses an ital axis for DM Serif Display and weight 800 for Nunito (app/pages/_document.tsx:9-11), while the proposed FontLoad summary omits or misstates those details (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:43).

User-uploaded or runtime themes would require a major revision, despite the manifest being kept serializable for later (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:30-32). The proposed architecture relies on static generated imports, generated AppThemeName, and value-in-APP_THEMES normalization (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19,39; app/src/lib/theme.ts:737-742).

A theme-specific icon variation beyond line/filled/pixel will force changes to schema, manifests, docs, and treatments. The current ThemeIcon fallback is hardcoded around pixel/toy/default (app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx:12-73,100), and the proposed IconSetName union is only line|filled|pixel (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:17).

A requirement to hide experimental themes from the picker while keeping them build-valid will stress the registry/profile contract. The manifest includes status? stable|experimental|hidden (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:82-83), but profile currently renders every Object.values(APP_THEMES) entry (app/pages/profile.tsx:221-241), and the proposal does not define filtering ownership.
blocking_concerns: AC9 has no enforceable baseline yet. The proposal admits A1 is unproven (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:55), and current tests are too narrow (app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts:4-33) for byte-identical Chakra output (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:159-160). Implementation can begin with no way to prove parity if this remains unresolved.

The public contracts contain contradictions that independent stories can implement differently: useThemeStyles unchanged vs visualStyle dropped (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:41; app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:325-330), manifest.iconSet vs manifest.treatments.iconSet (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:101; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:29,37), and optional auto-derived contentSurface vs consumers reading manifest.contentSurface (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:37-39; app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:340).

The build/test gate is not sufficient for a committed generated registry. A3 states make test-unit skips generation (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:57), and the repo confirms both Makefile and npm unit-test paths run vitest directly (Makefile:107-109; app/package.json:10). That allows stale registry.generated.ts to pass normal local checks.

THEME_FONTS is underspecified and already mismatched to the current font URL. This blocks AC14 parity because the proposal's font inventory differs from app/pages/_document.tsx:9-11 (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:43).

Registry ordering is unspecified even though APP_THEMES insertion order drives the profile picker (app/pages/profile.tsx:221) and current order is explicitly encoded in theme.ts (app/src/lib/theme.ts:677-733). A deterministic but different generated order can violate the no-redesign/pixel-identical migration goal (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:28-33).

The proposed type boundary is unresolved: types/index.ts currently owns AppThemeName as a pure type (app/src/types/index.ts:8), while the proposal permits types -> registry (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:27,49). That turns a global type module into a generated-theme consumer and creates a compile-time dependency on generated source before the generator story exists.
